text.title: Retention Benchmarks by Product Type
text.summary: Industry-standard D1/D7/D30/D90 retention benchmarks for B2B SaaS and consumer apps, presented as quality tiers (poor/average/good/great) to set realistic cohort targets.

rule.statement: Compare cohort retention against a benchmark band (poor/average/good/great), not a single target number — industry benchmarks shift yearly and vary by ICP segment.
rule.rationale: A single "target" number ignores the distribution; showing where you fall in the band is more actionable than pass/fail against an average.

rule.statement: Overlay benchmark bands on cohort retention charts as reference zones, not goal lines; a retention rate above the "average" band warrants celebration, below "poor" warrants immediate investigation.
rule.rationale: Static goal lines mislead when the product category or ICP shifts; bands acknowledge the distribution and give clearer signal about severity of deviations.

list.item: re-baseline benchmarks annually — D1 retention has shifted with iOS ATT and privacy changes
segment benchmarks by product type before comparing; B2B SaaS and consumer app benchmarks are not interchangeable
